channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Kashmir's Sufi music lovers are sticking with the audio cassette
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Farooq Ahmad Shaksaaz presses a button on his 1970 Sharp cassette player, and with a hefty clack the machine whirrs to life.
item.dc:creator: DAR YASIN and AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: M4 MacBook Air 2025 review: The perfect laptop for most consumers
item.description: Apple’s new MacBook Air laptops, which the company unveiled alongside a new iPad Air and a disappointing 11th-generation iPad earlier this month, are now on sale. They feature the M4 chip and some additional upgrades that remote workers and desk jockeys, in particular, may appreciate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Digital twin' danger
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI dashcams enhance trucker safety while raising privacy concerns
item.description: Dashcams that use artificial intelligence improve trucking safety and efficiency, but they also raise privacy concerns for drivers and pedestrians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: You can now watch a full Metallica concert on Apple Vision Pro
item.description: You don’t need tickets to see Metallica in concert anymore—just a $3,499 Apple Vision Pro headset. Starting today, Apple Vision Pro users can experience Metallica’s 2024 Mexico City concert as an immersive, ultrahigh resolution experience complete with 180-degree video and Spatial Audio.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX launches a new crew to the space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
item.description: The replacements for NASA's two stuck astronauts are on their way to the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google Messages could soon follow WhatsApp with an upgrade that makes it much easier to join group chats
item.description: Android Authority has spotted a line in Google Messages' beta that could make it easier for you to add people to group chats.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: You can now use an IPv4 address as business collateral - and it could be worth millions
item.description: You can now secure a loan using your IPv4 address as collateral – you’ll even still be able to access and use it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Rise of the Ronin Review: Poorly Optimized on High-End GPU | Gaming News
item.description: Koei Tecmo's Rise of the Ronin struggles to run on an RTX 4090 at 4K with constant stutters and slow-motion bugs. The game's poor optimization has left many players frustrated.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GitLab has patched a host of worrying security issues
item.description: Among the problems were two critical severity issues which allowed threat actors to bypass authentication.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Commerce secretary says Trump wants TikTok deal done under current deadline
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday said President Trump would like to hammer out a deal on TikTok's future ownership under the current deadline, saying the president "doesn't like to ask for extensions." "So, it's in Donald Trump's hands.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Women in tech statistics: The hard truths of an uphill battle
item.description: Diversity is critical to IT performance. Diverse teams perform better, hire better talent, have more engaged members, and retain workers better than those that don’t focus on diversity and inclusion, according to a recent report from McKinsey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Robotaxis are racking up tickets in San Francisco
item.description: San Francisco is a hot spot for Waymo vehicles, which operate without a human driver, but it seems the cars don't always play by the rules.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: What is Llama? Meta AI’s family of large language models explained
item.description: Llama, a family of sort-of open-source large language models (LLMs), was introduced in February 2023, and has been updated periodically ever since. Llama shook up the world of LLMs, which had previously been dominated by ever-larger closed-source models from OpenAI and Google.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Social media addicts: Here are tips to stop wasting time on apps
item.description: If you’ve got a smartphone, you probably spend too much time on it — checking Instagram, watching silly TikTok videos, messaging on WhatsApp or doomscrolling on X. It can be hard to curb excessive use of smartphones and social media, which are addictive by design.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How Wasm reinvents web development
item.description: WebAssembly fascinates me. The notion of assembly language is as old as computing. Typically, assembly meant specific instructions for moving data around inside of a physical central processor. Everything in a computer is ones and zeros, but a layer above that is assembly code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Designing a dynamic web application with Astro
item.description: Astro.js is among the most popular meta-frameworks for JavaScript developers, and for good reason. Astro makes it relatively easy to structure web applications for maximum effect with minimal effort.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
